next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 13

The road had been bad for two days. Not road — packed earth over old Shaping work, the kind of infrastructure the Hoarded contracted fast in the Margins and never maintained. Kem could feel it through his boots: shallow subsidence where structural charms had eroded, cracks running through compacted dirt, root systems pushing up from plants that should have been dead years ago but kept going anyway because nothing in the Margins died cleanly.

He was at forty-three.

Seven levels gone in two days. He could account for each one. A Light ability in a stretch of collapsed culvert on the first evening, where even Dren couldn't navigate the rubble blind. Two levels on a healing touch for a woman they'd found sitting in the brush with a broken wrist, cradling the arm in her lap like something she was carrying for someone else — she'd been there two days, she said, which meant two days of nobody coming, which meant the Margins. A Warmth Charm on a night when the basin wind found every seam in their camp. A minor Strength boost to help Dren shift a fallen crossbeam blocking the only passable track, because Dren's bad arm couldn't take the weight alone and going around would have cost them half a day they didn't have. Two more on a Flame Charm and a Water Purification when they found a silted well and no dry kindling.

Seven levels. Some of them he could have saved. The Warmth Charm, maybe — Vel could have rigged a windbreak. The Strength boost — they could have gone around. But the woman's wrist had been broken and nobody was coming, and the well water would have given...
open_hooks_to_carry: The Margins as exploited territory — indentured labor, debt-spending, depleted communities — rendered through bureaucratic dark comedy: Productivity Tiers, 'Renewable Expenditure Units,' interest rates on ability-debt
Fallow's unsentimental, darkly funny testimony: 'They called it a Development Opportunity. Thirty years of development. Very developed.'
The Scour identified as the threshold's geographic location

Kem's major spending: healing a sick child (8 levels), breaking an enforcement mechanism (20 levels), combat with enforcers (35 levels), helping repair infrastructure (15 levels), smaller interventions (20 levels) — 148 to 50, every spend dramatized as a moral choice
Vel watching Kem burn through her cascade budget and unable to tell him to stop, because stopping is what the Hoarded do
Maren's route journal consulted for the path to the Scour — Maren came through this region
prev_tail_excerpt:
...for forty-seven years. She had organized her career, her identity, and her understanding of what a person was around the principle that keeping was strength and spending was erosion.

But she was more capable than she had ever been. She could build a fire, read terrain, walk a full day on bad ground, make decisions without a committee, and sleep on the earth. She could track a man across a landscape she had never seen. None of these capacities appeared on a Ledger. All of them were real.

She reached for the institutional frame out of habit, the way she reached for the striker when she needed fire.

*Preliminary finding: expenditure of levels correlates with increased functional capacity in the field. This observation is inconsistent with the Council's preservation framework. Recommend further assessment.*

The language sat on top of the real question like a report cover on an audit. Keeping the contents contained, presentable, deniable.

She let the cover slide.

*Does spending make you more?*

She did not write it in her field notes. She did not file it in the observation log she had been maintaining out of bureaucratic reflex. She sat with the fire she had built and the calluses on her hands and the number 270 that was hers, and she let the question remain where it was — unprocessed, uncategorized, without a protocol for what to do next.

The fire cracked. A log shifted and sent a constellation of sparks upward into the dark. She watched them rise and vanish and did not reach for an ability to track their path, though she had one that would have worked. She let them go.
secondary_character_beats: Dren's silence speaks — a man at 130 looking at people at 4 and doing subtraction on his own life; he decides to continue for non-transactional reasons after watching Kem spend for strangers
Fallow's account is matter-of-fact: they have seen people vanish and it is another thing that happens to people nobody protects

setups_to_plant.description: The Margins' moral reality — involuntary depletion, systemic exploitation — becomes a pressure that contaminates subsequent confrontations. Vel's heroic-spending narrative and Kem's liberation-through-burning narrative are both complicated.

state_deltas_required: The visible human cost of Hoarded exploitation rendered through bureaucratic absurdity; Kem's restraint lesson colliding with moral necessity

The moral weight of the Ledger system becomes concrete through dark comedy; the Scour identified; Kem's 98-level spending spree dramatizes the cost of conscience; Dren makes his first non-transactional choice
